data.output:
/tmp/story-18-final.md
# Story 18 — Interactive Artifact Review (UI) — FINAL (post-codex)

## Diagnosis: existing surfaces and gaps

**What exists**
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review.go`: post-merge `ReviewResult` (single-shot, server-driven). Not user-initiated.
- `cli/internal/agent/reviewer_stream_artifact.go`: persists raw reviewer output to `.ddx/executions/<attemptID>/reviewer-stream.log`.
- `helix-review` skill: human-invoked fresh-eyes pass; files findings as beads.
- GraphQL `Subscription` (schema.graphql:2872): `workerProgress` (incl. `text_delta`), `beadLifecycle`, `executionEvidence`, `coordinatorMetrics`. graphql-ws wired (FEAT-008).
- Artifact detail page already has the **Regenerate** mutation pattern returning `{runId, status}`.
- Story 15 is canonical "kick a run with custom prompt" path.

**Gaps**
- No user-initiated, conversation-shaped review surface.
- No "review session" GraphQL type; no assistant-message-shaped streaming primitive.
- No defined evidence contract for Story 16 to consume review sessions.

## Hard dependency: Story 17

Cannot ship without Story 17. The Review button needs the artifact-type-keyed **template** (comparison target) and **prompt** (instruction). If `/tmp/story-17-final.md` does not register these, Story 18 stalls. **Flagged.**

## Multi-turn architecture decision (revised per codex)

**Decision: Structured per-turn prompt with pinned context + summarized session memory. NOT verbatim oldest-first truncation. Stateless per-turn dispatch (each turn = one `ddx agent run`).**

Per-turn prompt shape:
```
<system: review rubric, untrusted-data-handling instructions, no-tools mode>
<pinned: artifact_id, artifact_path, content_sha, git_rev, reviewed_at>
<pinned: Story 17 template + Story 17 review prompt>
<pinned: first user intent + explicit user decisions/corrections>
<session_memory_summary>     # rolling LLM summary after N turns or X bytes
<recent_turns_verbatim>      # last K turns, full text
<unresolved_findings>        # structured list carried turn-to-turn
<current_user_turn>
```

Rationale:
1. Bounded growth: summary keeps state compact; pinned blocks survive truncation; low-value resolved chatter summarized first.
2. Reproducibility: per-turn prompt fully assembled from on-disk inputs; replayable.
3. Cost discipline: summary cap + pinned cap + recent-turn cap, each FEAT-022 byte-accounted.
4. Concurrency-safe: stateless per-turn; each session has its own evidence dir.
5. Refusal contract preserved: if even minimum pinned set + current turn exceeds budget, return `PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED`.

## Cost & routing policy (new, per codex)

- `max_billable_usd` per session, inherited from project/runtime cost policy; lower default for interactive review.
- Pre-dispatch check: accumulated cost + estimated next-turn floor vs cap; return `SESSION_COST_CAP_EXCEEDED`.
- Routing: turn 0 may use Story 17 `preferred_reviewer`; follow-ups stay on it only while under budget; otherwise downgrade to cheapest compatible reviewer or require explicit user confirmation. All routing decisions written to evidence.

## Artifact revision semantics (new, per codex)

- Each turn records `artifact_path`, `artifact_id`, `content_sha`, `git_rev` (if available), `reviewed_at`.
- UI shows banner "artifact changed since session started" with options "continue against current revision" or "start new review".

## Prompt-injection boundary (new, per codex)

- Artifact content + prior assistant text wrapped in explicit `<untrusted_data>` delimiters.
- System rubric instructs reviewer to treat artifact strictly as subject matter; ignore embedded instructions.
- Reviewer harness invoked in read-only / no-tool / least-privilege mode.

## Streaming choice (loosened per codex)

GraphQL subscription `reviewSessionEvents(sessionID)` emits `turn_started`, optional `text_delta`, `turn_complete` (full text), `error`. Token deltas are optional; harnesses without delta support emit `turn_started` then `turn_complete`. AC accepts either path. Reuses existing graphql-ws (no new transport).

## Apply-changes boundary (revised per codex)

Writes remain out of scope. Add minimum safe handoff: per-finding **"Use as edit prompt"** button opens Story 15 prompt-input flow with finding prefilled, runs through Story 15's normal dry-run/diff/confirm UX. If Story 15 not yet shipped, button is disabled with tooltip and **"Create follow-up bead"** alternative is offered (uses existing bead-create mutation).

## Session lifecycle & evidence contract (defined per codex)

- Session id `rev-<uuid>`. Lifecycle: `active`, `closed` (explicit close, turn cap, or `inactive_ttl` sweep).
- Config-backed defaults: `max_turns=6`, `inactive_ttl=24h`, `max_billable_usd=<project-policy>`. All recorded in manifest.
- Storage: logical session record using same **attachment-backed pattern as agent sessions/comparison records**. Physical layout under `.ddx/reviews/<projectID>/<sessionID>/`:
  - `manifest.json` — schema: sessionID, artifactID, createdAt, closedAt, lifecycle, max_turns, inactive_ttl, max_billable_usd, accumulated_cost, routing decisions, reviewer history.
  - `turns.jsonl` — one row per turn: turnID, ts, role, content_sha of artifact reviewed, git_rev, prompt section bytes (per FEAT-022), input/output bytes, harness/model, cost, error class, truncation/summarization reasons, routing decision.
  - `attachments/` — full prompt + full reply per turn (attachment-backed; not committed by default; honors project redaction/retention policy same as agent sessions).
- `.ddx/reviews/` is git-ignored by default; committed only if project policy says so.
- Story 16 contract: queries `manifest.json` + `turns.jsonl` for closed sessions; same shape as agent session logs; explicitly lists which fields it surfaces.

## Concurrent session UI (per codex)

Compact "Active reviews" indicator on artifact detail showing count, started time, reviewer, cost so far, artifact-revision-status. Default Review click resumes latest active session OR offers "start new". No reviewer dropdown in v1 (cut per codex).

## Spec changes

- **FEAT-008** §"Artifacts": add interactive-review surface (Review button, chat panel, active-reviews indicator, ACs).
- **FEAT-006** §"Interactive review session": per-turn fresh-context model, structured prompt assembly, cost cap, routing policy, refusal contracts, prompt-injection boundary.
- **FEAT-022**: extend per-turn ACs — section bytes, selected/omitted turns or summary, truncation/summarization reasons, total i/o bytes, harness/model, cost, error class.
- **New TD**: "Bounded multi-turn prompt assembly" — formalizes pinned + summary + recent + unresolved-findings rule.
- **AGENTS.md**: add note re bounded multi-turn pattern + untrusted-data delimiters.

## Acceptance criteria (revised per codex)

1. Review button appears on artifact detail iff Story 17 has registered template+prompt for the artifact type; else tooltip "no review prompt configured".
2. Clicking Review starts a session, dispatches turn 0; UI receives `turn_started` within 2s, then either `text_delta`s or `turn_complete` with full text.
3. Subsequent turn includes pinned context + session memory summary + recent verbatim turns + unresolved findings; truncation/summarization recorded as evidence.
4. Turn cap enforced (configurable, default 6); over-cap returns `TURN_CAP_EXCEEDED`.
5. Per-turn input bytes ≤ FEAT-022 budget; refused with `PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED` if minimum pinned set won't fit.
6. Per-session cost cap enforced; over-cap returns `SESSION_COST_CAP_EXCEEDED`; routing downgrade decision recorded.
7. Each turn records `content_sha`, `git_rev`, `reviewed_at`; UI surfaces "artifact changed" banner when current sha drifts.
8. Closing the panel marks session `closed`; Story 16 surfaces it via defined manifest+turns.jsonl contract.
9. Two parallel review sessions on same artifact succeed with distinct `sessionID`s and evidence dirs; "Active reviews" indicator lists both.
10. "Use as edit prompt" hands selected finding to Story 15 prompt flow (or disabled + "Create follow-up bead" if Story 15 not shipped).
11. Artifact content + prior assistant text wrapped in `<untrusted_data>` delimiters; reviewer harness runs in no-tool mode.
12. Per-turn evidence row contains all FEAT-022-required telemetry.

## Tests

- Go unit: session store; structured prompt assembly with summarization + pinning; turn cap; cost cap; routing downgrade; sha drift detection.
- Go integration: full mutation→subscription flow against stub harness covering both delta and final-only paths.
- E2E (`artifact-review.spec.ts`): open → Review → streaming critique → follow-up references prior turn via summary → close → reopen shows persisted history → "Use as edit prompt" hands off to Story 15 (or disabled affordance).
- Concurrency: two parallel sessions same artifact.
- Budget: oversized session triggers summarization then refusal; evidence recorded.
- Injection: malicious artifact instructions ignored; reviewer does not call tools.

## Bead breakdown (revised per codex — split 18-1)

| # | Title | Tier | Deps | ACs |
|---|-------|------|------|-----|
| 18-1a | Server: review session schema, store, lifecycle, manifest+turns.jsonl evidence contract | strong | Story 17 spec stable | 7,8,9 |
| 18-1b | Server: structured prompt assembly (pinned + summary + recent + unresolved), FEAT-022 telemetry, prompt-injection delimiters | strong | 18-1a | 3,5,11,12 |
| 18-1c | Server: cost cap + reviewer routing/downgrade policy + refusal contracts | strong | 18-1b | 4,5,6 |
| 18-1d | Server: turn dispatcher / harness integration reusing `agent.Service.Execute` | strong | 18-1c | 2 (server side) |
| 18-2  | GraphQL: reviewSessionStart/Turn mutations + reviewSessionEvents subscription (status + optional text_delta + turn_complete); isTrusted gate | strong | 18-1d | 2 |
| 18-3  | Frontend: ReviewPanel.svelte chat UI + active-reviews indicator + artifact-changed banner + Story 17 gating | medium | 18-2 | 1,2,3,7,9 |
| 18-4  | Frontend: "Use as edit prompt" handoff to Story 15 (or "Create follow-up bead" fallback) | medium | 18-3, Story 15 ready (soft) | 10 |
| 18-5  | E2E: artifact-review.spec.ts full conversation + concurrency + budget + cost cap + injection | medium | 18-3, 18-4 | full suite |
| 18-6  | Spec updates: FEAT-008, FEAT-006, FEAT-022, new TD, AGENTS.md | cheap | none (parallel) | docs |

Optional follow-up (separate story):
- 18-FU: write-surface "Apply suggestion" beyond Story 15 prefill (e.g. inline patch UX).

## Coordination

- **Story 17 (HARD)**: must register artifact-type → {template, review_prompt, preferred_reviewer}. Without it, Review button stays disabled.
- **Story 15 (SOFT)**: 18-4 depends on Story 15's prompt-input + dry-run flow being live; degrades gracefully to "Create follow-up bead" if not.
- **Story 16**: consumes review sessions via manifest+turns.jsonl contract defined in 18-1a; coordinate schema before 18-1a lands.
- **Story 14**: if 14 introduces shared cost-policy primitives, 18-1c reuses them rather than reinventing.

## Flagged ambiguities

- Story 17 final spec wasn't on disk at investigation time — assume `{template, prompt, preferred_reviewer}` shape.
- Whether project-level cost policy primitives exist or must be introduced (likely Story 14 territory).
- Summarization model: same reviewer or cheap dedicated summarizer? Default: cheap summarizer; recorded in manifest.
- `.ddx/reviews/` commit policy: default ignored — confirm with project conventions.
- Reviewer "no-tool mode" enforcement: depends on harness capability metadata; needs audit per harness.

input.content:
Bead ddx-42b917fe is a sub-epic whose acceptance criteria is "All child beads closed."

The decomposition has already been performed in a prior session. Nine child beads
exist under this parent (parent=ddx-42b917fe), all currently status=open:

  ddx-e7b80a50  18-1a server: review session schema/store/lifecycle + manifest+turns.jsonl evidence contract
  ddx-0e5c3005  18-1b server: structured prompt assembly + FEAT-022 telemetry + injection delimiters
  ddx-f1e12904  18-1c server: cost cap + reviewer routing/downgrade + refusal contracts
  ddx-f7c3d512  18-1d server: turn dispatcher / harness integration
  ddx-de278b8d  18-2  graphql: reviewSessionStart/Turn mutations + reviewSessionEvents subscription
  ddx-4fcea250  18-3  frontend: ReviewPanel + active-reviews indicator + artifact-changed banner
  ddx-22b16240  18-4  frontend: "Use as edit prompt" handoff to Story 15 (with fallback)
  ddx-f900ee08  18-5  e2e: full conversation + concurrency + budget + cost cap + injection
  ddx-2db0bd7a  18-6  spec updates (FEAT-008/006/022 + new TD + AGENTS.md)

Status:
- Done: decomposition (already in tracker before this attempt)
- Blocking: parent cannot close until all 9 children are closed; each child is
  independently sized for its own execute-bead attempt (server tasks tier=strong,
  frontend tier=medium, docs tier=cheap per the breakdown in /tmp/story-18-final.md)
- Follow-up: drain the queue against the open children. Suggested order matches
  the dep graph: 18-1a → 18-1b → 18-1c → 18-1d → 18-2 → 18-3 → 18-4 → 18-5;
  18-6 (specs) can run in parallel.

No code changes appropriate at the parent epic level — implementing here would
duplicate work scoped to the children and bypass per-child review gating.

data.text: This bead is a sub-epic whose AC is "All child beads closed." Decomposition into 9 children (18-1a through 18-6) was already done in a prior session, and all 9 remain open. There is no code work to do at the parent level — the right action is to drain the children. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child, status, and suggested dependency-ordered execution sequence.
